title: Getting started with `{hazrd}`
text: package ships simulated survival dataset, test_data. three required columns phs (continuous score), age (time event censoring), status (event indicator: 1 = event, 0 = censored). test data included package.

title: Getting started with `{hazrd}`
text: phs_metrics() single entry point discrimination statistics. fits Cox proportional hazards model derives metrics fit. metrics argument controls statistics computed; column names passed strings default "phs", "age", "status".

what: All core metrics in one call
title: Getting started with `{hazrd}`
text: returned tibble one row per metric. conf_low, conf_high, se NA bootstrapping enabled.
code: metrics <- phs_metrics(   test_data,   metrics  = c("HR", "C_index", "OR", "HR_SD"),   or_age   = 70 ) metrics #> # A tibble: 4 × 9 #>   metric      estimate conf_low conf_high    se n_numerator n_denominator method #>   <chr>          <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl>       <int>         <int> <chr>  #> 1 HR[80-100]…    8.17        NA        NA    NA         200           200 conti… #> 2 C_index        0.708       NA        NA    NA          NA            NA harre… #> 3 HR_SD          2.12        NA        NA    NA          NA            NA NA     #> 4 OR[80-100]…    8.52        NA        NA    NA         200           200 NA     #> # ℹ 1 more variable: adjusted <lgl>

title: Getting started with `{hazrd}`
text: default phs_metrics() computes HR[80-100]_[0-20] — ratio top 20% bottom 20% PHS distribution. Use hr_numerator / hr_denominator change bands: multiple HRs one call, supply hr_pairs:
code: phs_metrics(test_data, metrics = "HR", hr_numerator = 0.90, hr_denominator = 0.10) #> # A tibble: 1 × 9 #>   metric      estimate conf_low conf_high    se n_numerator n_denominator method #>   <chr>          <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl>       <int>         <int> <chr>  #> 1 HR[90-100]…     13.7       NA        NA    NA         100           100 conti… #> # ℹ 1 more variable: adjusted <lgl> phs_metrics(   test_data,   metrics  = "HR",   hr_pairs = list(     list(numerator = c(0.80, 1.00), denominator = c(0.00, 0.20)),     list(numerator = c(0.80, 1.00), denominator = c(0.40, 0.60))   ) ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 9 #>   metric      estimate conf_low conf_high    se n_numerator n_denominator method #>   <chr>          <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl>       <int>         <int> <chr>  #> 1 HR[80-100]…     8.17       NA        NA    NA         200           200 conti… #> 2 HR[80-100]…     3.05       NA        NA    NA         200           200 conti… #> # ℹ 1 more variable: adjusted <lgl>

title: Getting started with `{hazrd}`
text: computed Kaplan-Meier survival estimates specific age. or_age accepts vector; one row returned per age:
code: phs_metrics(test_data, metrics = "OR", or_age = c(65, 70, 75)) #> # A tibble: 3 × 9 #>   metric      estimate conf_low conf_high    se n_numerator n_denominator method #>   <chr>          <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl>       <int>         <int> <chr>  #> 1 OR[80-100]…     8.22       NA        NA    NA         200           200 NA     #> 2 OR[80-100]…     8.52       NA        NA    NA         200           200 NA     #> 3 OR[80-100]…    19.1        NA        NA    NA         200           200 NA     #> # ℹ 1 more variable: adjusted <lgl>

title: Getting started with `{hazrd}`
text: Set bootstrap = TRUE populate conf_low, conf_high, se. Use small n_boot speed development:
code: phs_metrics(   test_data,   metrics   = c("HR", "C_index", "HR_SD"),   bootstrap = TRUE,   n_boot    = 300,   seed      = 42 ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 9 #>   metric     estimate conf_low conf_high     se n_numerator n_denominator method #>   <chr>         <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>  <dbl>       <int>         <int> <chr>  #> 1 HR[80-100…    8.17     5.93     11.8   1.36           200           200 conti… #> 2 C_index       0.708    0.680     0.734 0.0139          NA            NA harre… #> 3 HR_SD         2.12     1.90      2.42  0.124           NA            NA NA     #> # ℹ 1 more variable: adjusted <lgl>

title: Getting started with `{hazrd}`
text: Column names passed strings, making phs_metrics() straightforward use non-standard data frames:
code: test_data2 <- with(test_data, data.frame(   score     = phs,   diagnosis_age = age,   case      = status ))  phs_metrics(   test_data2,   phs   = "score",   time  = "diagnosis_age",   event = "case",   metrics = c("HR", "C_index") ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 9 #>   metric      estimate conf_low conf_high    se n_numerator n_denominator method #>   <chr>          <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl>       <int>         <int> <chr>  #> 1 HR[80-100]…    8.17        NA        NA    NA         200           200 conti… #> 2 C_index        0.708       NA        NA    NA          NA            NA harre… #> # ℹ 1 more variable: adjusted <lgl>

title: Getting started with `{hazrd}`
text: phs_km_curve() stratifies cohort percentile bands plots empirical Kaplan-Meier survival curves band. returns either ggplot object (output = "plot", default) tidy data frame (output = "data").

title: Getting started with `{hazrd}`
text: default breaks = c(0.20, 0.80) splits cohort bottom 20%, middle 60%, top 20%:
code: phs_km_curve(test_data) #> Warning: Removed 2 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_ribbon()`).

title: Getting started with `{hazrd}`
text: Pass numeric vector percentile cutpoints (strictly (0, 1)) breaks:
code: # Quintiles phs_km_curve(test_data, breaks = c(0.20, 0.40, 0.60, 0.80)) #> Warning: Removed 2 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_ribbon()`).

title: Getting started with `{hazrd}`
text: phs_km_curve() returns standard ggplot object can extended ggplot2 layers, themes, scales:
code: phs_km_curve(test_data) +   theme_classic(base_size = 13) +   labs(     title = "Disease-free Survival by PHS Percentile",     x     = "Age (years)",     y     = "Disease-free Probability",     color = "PHS Group",     fill  = "PHS Group"   ) +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(40, 100)) #> Warning: Removed 2 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_ribbon()`).

title: Getting started with `{hazrd}`
text: Use output = "data" want build fully custom plot pass survival estimates analysis: returned data frame columns time, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, n.risk, n.event, stratum, can used directly ggplot2:
code: km_data <- phs_km_curve(test_data, output = "data") head(km_data) #>       time estimate conf.low conf.high n.risk n.event stratum #> 1 42.92288        1        1         1    199       0   0-20% #> 2 43.64664        1        1         1    198       0   0-20% #> 3 44.88269        1        1         1    197       0   0-20% #> 4 47.17180        1        1         1    196       0   0-20% #> 5 48.21010        1        1         1    195       0   0-20% #> 6 48.52730        1        1         1    194       0   0-20% ggplot(km_data, aes(x = time, y = estimate,                     ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high,                     color = stratum, fill = stratum)) +   geom_ribbon(alpha = 0.15, color = NA) +   geom_step(linewidth = 0.8) +   scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "PHS Group") +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "PHS Group") +   coord_cartesian(xlim = c(40, 100), ylim = c(0, 1)) +   labs(x = "Age (years)", y = "Disease-free Probability") +   theme_minimal() #> Warning: Removed 2 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_ribbon()`).

title: Getting started with `{hazrd}`
text: phs_cox_curve() fits Cox proportional-hazards model (phs sole predictor) returns predicted survival curves individuals specified PHS percentiles. Unlike phs_km_curve(), smooth model-based predictions, empirical group estimates.  Supply specific percentiles via percentiles argument:
code: phs_cox_curve(test_data) phs_cox_curve(test_data, percentiles = c(0.01, 0.10, 0.50, 0.90, 0.99))

title: Authors and Citation
text: Andrew Morris. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Morris (2026). hazrd: Polygenic Hazard Score Cox Proportional Hazards Model. R package version 0.2.0, https://amorris28.github.io/hazrd/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {hazrd: Polygenic Hazard Score with Cox Proportional Hazards Model},   author = {Andrew Morris},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.0},   url = {https://amorris28.github.io/hazrd/}, }

title: NA
text: repository R package named hazrd focused evaluating visualizing polygenic hazard scores (PHS) using survival models. Key components live R/ directory tests use testthat/. Files scan first - R/phs_metrics.R — user-facing metric orchestrator (HR, C-index, ) - R/metrics-internal.R — metric implementations (._calc_* functions) - R/bootstrap-internal.R — bootstrapping orchestration; uses boot::boot - R/create_lookup.R — generation per-percentile K-M predictions - tests/testthat/ — unit tests validate behavior - DESCRIPTION, NAMESPACE, README.Rmd — packaging install metadata - Never edit README.md directly — auto-generated knitting README.Rmd via rmarkdown::render(). Always use Docker container pandoc: docker run --rm -v /path//hazrd:/repo -r-image \       Rscript -e 'rmarkdown::render("/repo/README.Rmd", output_file = "/repo/README.md")' use knitr::knit() — lacks pandoc emits YAML frontmatter literal text output. - docs/specs/spec-sheet.md — package spec sheet (may local/ignored; ask maintainer copy) Big-picture architecture & data flow - Input: data.frame minimum phs, age (time) status (event). - phs_metrics() single primary entry-point computing metrics; fits Cox model (survival::coxph) derives metrics fit. - Metric calculation split: point estimates computed first; bootstrap = TRUE package calls .run_bootstrap() re-fits model resamples derives bootstrap CIs using boot::boot + boot.ci. - Outputs tidy tibbles canonical columns: metric, estimate, conf_low, conf_high, se, n_numerator, n_denominator, method, adjusted. Developer spec - See docs/specs/spec-sheet.md canonical package specification. NOTE: file may kept locally git-ignored maintainer. find repo, ask maintainer provide Markdown copy. spec documents package goals, input conventions (string column names), metric naming conventions (e.g. HR[80-100]_[0-20]), bootstrap behavior (internal boot::boot usage CI methods), worked examples phs_metrics, phs_km, phs_abs_risk. Project-specific conventions & patterns (important edits) - Column names usually passed strings (e.g. phs = "phs", time = "age", event = "status"). Functions accept string names often index data data[[phs]] rather non-standard-eval. - HR metric naming convention literal parsed elsewhere: e.g. HR[80-100]_[0-20] — tests bootstrapping rely exact names align CI results back rows. - Bootstrapping: parallel argument present "" implemented (see .run_bootstrap() guard). Avoid changing parallel semantics without updating guard tests. - Many internal helpers return tibbles canonical column order; keep contract adding new metrics. Build / test / debug workflows (commands can run locally) - Install dev version: R: devtools::install("/path//hazrd") devtools::build() install.packages(..., repos = NULL, type = "source"). - Run package tests: R: devtools::test() shell: R -e ‘devtools::test()’ - Run R CMD check: repo root: R CMD check –-cran . - repo GitHub Action R-CMD-check.yaml (see badge README.md) — mimic action’s R versions reproducing CI locally. Integration points & external deps - Declared imports DESCRIPTION: boot, dplyr, ggplot2, survival, tibble, tidyr, scales. Use consistent idioms. - boot::boot boot::boot.ci central — changes bootstrap interface must preserve numeric-vector contract used boot. Testing & safety notes code changes - Add unit tests tests/testthat/ change metric logic bootstrap behavior. package uses testthat edition 3. - Keep reported metric names stable: tests bootstrap matching rely exact metric name strings. - modifying exported functions, update roxygen comments R/ source — run devtools::document() regenerate NAMESPACE man/ files. Good example edits get started - Small bugfix: ensure or_age bounds handled (see .calc_or_metric) — add test covering or_age outside observed range. - Add new C-index method: implement "uno" .calc_cindex_metric() add test example R/phs_metrics.R documentation. Prompting hints AI agent - change metric output shape, list exact column names show one-row example tibble maintainers can verify compatibility. - bootstrap changes, include minimal reproducible call phs_metrics bootstrap = TRUE n_boot = 50 tests CI runs fast. anything file unclear ’d like detail (examples expected inputs, sample dev/test script, CI matrix notes), tell section ’ll expand .

what: Polygenic Hazard Score with Cox Proportional Hazards Model
title: Polygenic Hazard Score with Cox Proportional Hazards Model
text: hazrd R package evaluating visualizing Polygenic Hazard Score (PHS) analyses. provides consistent, tidy-first API computing survival-based discrimination metrics, bootstrapped confidence intervals, Kaplan-Meier curves.

title: Polygenic Hazard Score with Cox Proportional Hazards Model
text: install recent release Github, go release page, scroll Assets, download “Source code (tar.gz)” file. , R, run:
code: install.packages("hazrd-0.2.0.tar.gz", repos = NULL, type = "source")

title: Polygenic Hazard Score with Cox Proportional Hazards Model
text: development version:
code: git clone git@github.com:amorris28/hazrd.git devtools::install("hazrd")

title: Polygenic Hazard Score with Cox Proportional Hazards Model
text: full introduction see vignette hazrd pkgdown website.

title: Polygenic Hazard Score with Cox Proportional Hazards Model
code: library(hazrd)  # Compute HR, C-index, OR, and HR_SD in one call phs_metrics(   test_data,   metrics = c("HR", "C_index", "OR", "HR_SD"),   or_age  = 70)  # Bootstrapped confidence intervals phs_metrics(   test_data,   metrics   = c("HR", "C_index"),   bootstrap = TRUE,   n_boot    = 999,   seed      = 42 )  # Kaplan-Meier plot stratified by PHS percentile (returns a ggplot object) phs_km_curve(test_data)  # Return tidy data for a custom ggplot km_data <- phs_km_curve(test_data, breaks = c(0.20, 0.40, 0.60, 0.80), output = "data")

title: Polygenic Hazard Score with Cox Proportional Hazards Model
text: following roadmap future releases. Contributions welcome. phs_abs_risk() — absolute (cumulative incidence) risk curves competing risk support (km, aalen_johansen, fine_gray methods) phs_percentile() — compute percentile ranks; optionally relative reference population via ref_data phs_cut() — assign individuals percentile strata human-readable factor labels phs_describe() — descriptive statistics (n, events, median follow-, PHS mean/SD) stratum phs_calibration() — calibration plot Hosmer-Lemeshow statistic theme_phs() / scale_color_phs() — default ggplot2 theme colour scale hazrd plots Uno’s C-index — IPCW-weighted concordance heavily censored data (cindex_method = "uno" phs_metrics()) Parallel bootstrap — parallel = "multicore" / "snow" via boot::boot() (argument exists; "" currently active) Risk table — numbers--risk appended KM curves (risk_table = TRUE phs_km()) Alternative HR methods — continuous_point categorical (hr_method argument phs_metrics(); stubs exist)

what: Fit a polygenic hazard score (PHS) model — fit_phs
title: Fit a polygenic hazard score (PHS) model — fit_phs
text: fit_phs() fits PHS model either formula interface (similar coxph()) directly data frame specified columns. returns object class "phsfit", can passed get_hr(), get_or(), get_cindex(), etc.

title: Fit a polygenic hazard score (PHS) model — fit_phs
code: fit_phs(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'formula' fit_phs(object, data, bootstrap.iterations = NULL, conf.level = 0.95, ...)  # S3 method for class 'data.frame' fit_phs(   object,   age = "age",   status = "status",   phs = "phs",   bootstrap.iterations = NULL,   conf.level = 0.95,   ... )

title: Fit a polygenic hazard score (PHS) model — fit_phs
text: object Either survival formula, e.g. Surv(age, status) ~ phs (formula interface), data.frame containing variables phs, age, status (data-frame interface). ... Additional arguments (currently ignored). data data.frame containing variables formula (formula interface ). bootstrap.iterations Number bootstrap iterations run generate confidence intervals. conf.level confidence level use confidence interval conf.int = TRUE. Must strictly greater 0 less 1. Defaults 0.95, corresponds 95 percent confidence interval. age string specifying column name data containing age subject. cases, age event (e.g., diagnosis) controls age censoring (e.g., last observation). Default "age". status string specifying column name data containing case-control status (0 = censored, 1 = event). Default "status". phs string specifying column name data containing polygenic hazard score subject (data-frame interface ). Default "phs".

title: Fit a polygenic hazard score (PHS) model — fit_phs
text: object class "phsfit" containing base Cox model, bootstrap fits indices, formula, original data, bootstrap metadata.

title: Fit a polygenic hazard score (PHS) model — fit_phs
code: # Formula interface: fit <- fit_phs(Surv(age, status) ~ phs, data = test_data) #> Error in fit_phs(Surv(age, status) ~ phs, data = test_data): could not find function "fit_phs"  # Data frame interface: fit <- fit_phs(test_data, age = "age", status = "status", phs = "phs") #> Error in fit_phs(test_data, age = "age", status = "status", phs = "phs"): could not find function "fit_phs"

title: Deprecated: use phs_metrics() instead — get_cindex
text: get_cindex() deprecated hazrd 0.2.0. Please use phs_metrics() metrics = "C_index" instead.

title: Deprecated: use phs_metrics() instead — get_cindex
code: get_cindex(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'data.frame' get_cindex(   object,   phs = "phs",   age = "age",   status = "status",   bootstrap.iterations = NULL,   conf.level = 0.95,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'phsfit' get_cindex(object, bootstrap.iterations = NULL, conf.level = 0.95, ...)

title: Deprecated: use phs_metrics() instead — get_cindex
text: data data.frame containing PHS data phs column name PHS values. Default "phs" age column name time--event. Default "age" status column name event indicator. Default "status" bootstrap.iterations number bootstrap replicates, NULL conf.level confidence level. Default 0.95

title: Deprecated: use phs_metrics() instead — get_cindex
text: list element value optionally conf.low/conf.high.

title: Deprecated: use phs_metrics() instead — get_hr
text: get_hr() deprecated hazrd 0.2.0. Please use phs_metrics() metrics = "HR" instead.

title: Deprecated: use phs_metrics() instead — get_hr
code: get_hr(   data = NULL,   phs = "phs",   age = "age",   status = "status",   lower_interval = 0.2,   upper_interval = 0.8,   CI = FALSE,   bootstrap_iterations = 1000,   swc = FALSE,   swc_popnumcases = NULL,   swc_popnumcontrols = NULL )

title: Deprecated: use phs_metrics() instead — get_hr
text: data data.frame containing PHS data phs column name PHS values. Default "phs" age column name time--event. Default "age" status column name event indicator. Default "status" lower_interval single quantile (upper bound denominator band, lower bound defaults 0) length-2 vector c(lower, upper). Default 0.20 upper_interval single quantile (lower bound numerator band, upper bound defaults 1) length-2 vector c(lower, upper). Default 0.80 CI logical. TRUE, bootstrap CIs computed. Default FALSE bootstrap_iterations number bootstrap replicates. Default 1000 swc logical. Sample weight correction. supported phs_metrics() — warning emitted TRUE. Default FALSE swc_popnumcases ignored (swc yet implemented phs_metrics()) swc_popnumcontrols ignored (swc yet implemented phs_metrics())

title: Deprecated: use phs_metrics() instead — get_hr
text: list elements index, value, conf.low, conf.high, iters, matching return structure previous release.

TRUE, bootstrap CIs computed. Default FALSE conf.level confidence level. Default 0.95 bootstrap.iterations number bootstrap replicates. Required conf.int = TRUE

what: hazrd: Polygenic Hazard Score with Cox Proportional Hazards Model — hazrd-package
title: hazrd: Polygenic Hazard Score with Cox Proportional Hazards Model — hazrd-package
text: Tools evaluate visualize Polygenic Hazard Score (PHS) analyses. Provides consistent API computing survival-based discrimination metrics (hazard ratio, C-index, odds ratio, HR per SD), bootstrapped confidence intervals, Kaplan-Meier curves stratified PHS percentile group.

title: hazrd: Polygenic Hazard Score with Cox Proportional Hazards Model — hazrd-package
text: Maintainer: Andrew Morris andrewmorris@mailbox.org

title: Deprecated: use phs_km() instead — km_curve
text: km_curve() deprecated hazrd 0.2.0. Please use phs_km_curve() instead.

title: Deprecated: use phs_km() instead — km_curve
code: km_curve(object, ...)  # S3 method for class 'data.frame' km_curve(   object,   phs = "phs",   age = "age",   status = "status",   interval = c(0, 1),   age_range = 40:100,   scale = FALSE,   inverse = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'phsfit' km_curve(   object,   phs = "phs",   age = "age",   status = "status",   interval = c(0, 1),   age_range = 40:100,   scale = FALSE,   inverse = FALSE,   ... )

title: Deprecated: use phs_km() instead — km_curve
text: data data.frame containing PHS data phs column name PHS values. Default "phs" age column name time--event. Default "age" status column name event indicator. Default "status" interval numeric vector length 2 specifying lower upper quantiles filtering subjects. Default c(0, 1). direct equivalent phs_km() — warning emitted argument ignored; subjects included stratified output. age_range ages calculate curves. Default 40:100. supported phs_km() — warning emitted argument ignored. scale logical. TRUE, PHS centred scaled unit variance. supported phs_km() — warning emitted argument ignored. Scale PHS column calling phs_km(). inverse logical. TRUE, PHS multiplied -1. supported phs_km() — warning emitted argument ignored. Multiply PHS column manually calling phs_km().

title: Deprecated: use phs_km() instead — km_curve
text: data.frame returned phs_km_curve() output = "data".

what: Cox model survival curves at specified PHS percentiles — phs_cox_curve
title: Cox model survival curves at specified PHS percentiles — phs_cox_curve
text: Fit Cox proportional-hazards model phs sole predictor return predicted survival curves individuals specified PHS percentiles. Unlike phs_km_curve(), smooth model-based predictions rather empirical group estimates.

title: Cox model survival curves at specified PHS percentiles — phs_cox_curve
code: phs_cox_curve(   data,   phs = "phs",   time = "age",   event = "status",   percentiles = c(0.01, 0.05, 0.2, 0.5, 0.8, 0.95, 0.99),   ref_data = NULL,   output = "plot",   conf_int = TRUE,   conf_int_alpha = 0.15,   palette = "hazrd",   ... )

title: Cox model survival curves at specified PHS percentiles — phs_cox_curve
text: data data.frame columns specified phs, time, event phs string numeric vector; column name vector PHS values time string numeric vector; column name vector event times event string numeric vector; column name vector event indicators (0/1) percentiles numeric vector percentiles strictly (0, 1) compute Cox-predicted survival curves; default c(0.01, 0.05, 0.20, 0.50, 0.80, 0.95, 0.99) ref_data optional data.frame used compute PHS value requested percentile (training reference); NULL, percentiles computed data output 'plot' (default) 'data' conf_int logical; include confidence intervals output/plot conf_int_alpha numeric; alpha confidence ribbons plotting palette string; colour palette plots (default: 'hazrd') ... additional args (reserved)

title: Cox model survival curves at specified PHS percentiles — phs_cox_curve
text: ggplot object (output = 'plot') data.frame (output = 'data'). data frame columns time, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, percentile, percentile_value.

title: Cox model survival curves at specified PHS percentiles — phs_cox_curve
text: Percentile values computed ref_data supplied, supports training→validation workflow reference distribution comes training cohort.

what: Kaplan-Meier curves stratified by PHS percentile — phs_km_curve
title: Kaplan-Meier curves stratified by PHS percentile — phs_km_curve
text: Compute Kaplan-Meier survival curves stratified PHS percentile groups. Returns either ggplot object (output = "plot") tidy data frame (output = "data"). Preset strata custom cutpoints supported; percentiles computed ref_data supplied training->validation workflow possible.

title: Kaplan-Meier curves stratified by PHS percentile — phs_km_curve
code: phs_km_curve(   data,   phs = "phs",   time = "age",   event = "status",   breaks = c(0.2, 0.8),   ref_data = NULL,   output = "plot",   conf_int = TRUE,   conf_int_alpha = 0.15,   palette = "hazrd",   risk_table = FALSE,   ... )

title: Kaplan-Meier curves stratified by PHS percentile — phs_km_curve
text: data data.frame columns specified phs, time, event phs string numeric vector; column name vector PHS values time string numeric vector; column name vector event time event string numeric vector; column name vector event indicator (0/1) breaks numeric vector percentile cutpoints strictly (0, 1); default c(0.20, 0.80) (bottom 20% / middle 60% / top 20%) ref_data optional data.frame used compute percentile cutpoints (training reference) output 'plot' (default) 'data' conf_int logical; include confidence intervals output/plot conf_int_alpha numeric; alpha confidence ribbons plotting palette string; name color palette use plots (default: 'hazrd') risk_table logical; TRUE returns numbers--risk data output (experimental) ... additional args (reserved)

title: Kaplan-Meier curves stratified by PHS percentile — phs_km_curve
text: ggplot object (output = 'plot') data.frame (output = 'data')

what: Calculate evaluation metrics for Polygenic Hazard Scores — phs_metrics
title: Calculate evaluation metrics for Polygenic Hazard Scores — phs_metrics
text: Calculate evaluation metrics Polygenic Hazard Scores

title: Calculate evaluation metrics for Polygenic Hazard Scores — phs_metrics
code: phs_metrics(   data,   phs = "phs",   time = "age",   event = "status",   metrics = c("HR", "C_index"),   hr_method = "continuous_group",   hr_numerator = NULL,   hr_denominator = NULL,   hr_pairs = NULL,   cindex_method = "harrell",   or_age = NULL,   or_numerator = NULL,   or_denominator = NULL,   or_pairs = NULL,   bootstrap = FALSE,   n_boot = 999L,   ci_level = 0.95,   boot_method = "percentile",   seed = NULL,   parallel = "no",   n_cores = 1L,   strata = NULL )

title: Calculate evaluation metrics for Polygenic Hazard Scores — phs_metrics
text: data data.frame containing columns specified phs, time, event phs string specifying column name containing polygenic hazard score time string specifying column name containing time event censoring event string specifying column name containing event indicator (0 = censored, 1 = event) metrics character vector specifying metrics compute. Options "HR", "C_index", "HR_SD", "". hr_method method use HR calculation. One "continuous_group" (default), "continuous_point", "categorical". "continuous_group" currently implemented. hr_numerator numeric. lower boundary numerator group percentile (e.g., 0.80 top 20%). numerator group defined [hr_numerator, 1.0]. Ignored hr_pairs provided. Default 0.80. hr_denominator numeric. upper boundary denominator group percentile (e.g., 0.20 bottom 20%). denominator group defined [0.0, hr_denominator]. Ignored hr_pairs provided. Default 0.20. hr_pairs list HR specifications computing multiple HRs one call. element named list numerator denominator, length-2 numeric vector specifying [lower, upper] percentile boundaries band. combined hr_numerator/hr_denominator. Example: cindex_method method use C-index calculation. One "harrell" (default) "uno" (yet implemented). or_age integer numeric vector specifying age(s) odds ratio calculated. Required "" metrics. One row returned per age. or_numerator numeric. Lower boundary numerator band calculation. Default 0.80. or_denominator numeric. Upper boundary denominator band calculation. Default 0.20. or_pairs structure hr_pairs calculation. bootstrap logical. Whether compute bootstrapped confidence intervals. Default FALSE. TRUE, conf_low, conf_high, se populated returned tibble. n_boot integer. Number bootstrap replicates. Default 1000. ci_level numeric. Confidence level bootstrap CIs. Default 0.95. boot_method character. Method deriving CIs bootstrap distribution. One "percentile" (default), "bca" (bias-corrected accelerated), "normal". seed optional integer. Random seed reproducibility. parallel character. Parallelisation backend passed boot::boot(). One "" (default), "multicore", "snow". "" currently implemented. n_cores integer. Number cores parallel bootstrapping. Passed ncpus boot::boot(). Default 1L. strata optional string. Column name stratify resampling (preserves case/control ratio within level). Default NULL.
code: hr_pairs = list(     list(numerator = c(0.80, 1.00), denominator = c(0.00, 0.20)),     list(numerator = c(0.80, 1.00), denominator = c(0.40, 0.60))   )

title: Calculate evaluation metrics for Polygenic Hazard Scores — phs_metrics
text: tibble one row per metric columns: metric Full explicit metric name, e.g. "HR[80-100]_[0-20]", "C_index", "[80-100]_[0-20]_age70" estimate Point estimate computed full dataset conf_low Lower CI bound (NA bootstrap = FALSE) conf_high Upper CI bound (NA bootstrap = FALSE) se Bootstrap standard error (NA bootstrap = FALSE) n_numerator Sample size numerator group (HR ) n_denominator Sample size denominator group (HR ) method Method flag used, e.g. "continuous_group", "harrell"; NA adjusted Whether covariates used (always FALSE covariate support added)

title: Calculate evaluation metrics for Polygenic Hazard Scores — phs_metrics
text: hr_numerator hr_denominator convenience arguments common case single HR comparing top bottom PHS distribution. multiple HRs non-standard reference bands (e.g., middle reference group), use hr_pairs instead. Providing raise error.

title: Calculate evaluation metrics for Polygenic Hazard Scores — phs_metrics
text: bootstrap = TRUE, phs_metrics() calls boot::boot() internally. replicate Cox model re-fitted resampled data metrics derived (HR, HR_SD, C-index) jointly consistent. Failures individual replicates (e.g., degenerate resamples) produce NA replicate excluded SE/CI calculation warning.

title: Calculate evaluation metrics for Polygenic Hazard Scores — phs_metrics
code: # Simple case – HR[80-100]_[0-20] phs_metrics(test_data, metrics = "HR", hr_numerator = 0.80, hr_denominator = 0.20) #> # A tibble: 1 × 9 #>   metric      estimate conf_low conf_high    se n_numerator n_denominator method #>   <chr>          <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl>       <int>         <int> <chr>  #> 1 HR[80-100]…     8.17       NA        NA    NA         200           200 conti… #> # ℹ 1 more variable: adjusted <lgl>  # Multiple HRs with custom bands phs_metrics(test_data, metrics = "HR",   hr_pairs = list(     list(numerator = c(0.80, 1.00), denominator = c(0.00, 0.20)),     list(numerator = c(0.80, 1.00), denominator = c(0.40, 0.60))   )) #> # A tibble: 2 × 9 #>   metric      estimate conf_low conf_high    se n_numerator n_denominator method #>   <chr>          <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl>       <int>         <int> <chr>  #> 1 HR[80-100]…     8.17       NA        NA    NA         200           200 conti… #> 2 HR[80-100]…     3.05       NA        NA    NA         200           200 conti… #> # ℹ 1 more variable: adjusted <lgl>  # With bootstrapped CIs phs_metrics(test_data, metrics = c("HR", "C_index"),   bootstrap = TRUE, n_boot = 500, seed = 42) #> # A tibble: 2 × 9 #>   metric     estimate conf_low conf_high     se n_numerator n_denominator method #>   <chr>         <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>  <dbl>       <int>         <int> <chr>  #> 1 HR[80-100…    8.17     5.95     11.4   1.36           200           200 conti… #> 2 C_index       0.708    0.680     0.734 0.0136          NA            NA harre… #> # ℹ 1 more variable: adjusted <lgl>

title: hazrd 0.2.0
text: phs_metrics() — single entry point discrimination metrics (HR, C-index, , HR_SD). Returns tidy tibble one row per metric. Supports built-bootstrapping via boot::boot() "percentile", "bca", "normal" CI methods. phs_km() — Kaplan-Meier curves stratified PHS percentile group. Returns ggplot object default (output = "plot") tidy data frame (output = "data"). Includes seven stratification presets (quantile_3, quantile_4, quantile_5, quantile_10, clinical, binary_80_20, binary_top_bottom) custom_cuts override. Supports ref_data argument train/validation percentile workflows.

title: hazrd 0.2.0
text: following functions deprecated 0.2.0 removed 0.3.0. continue work emit deprecation warning pointing replacement call. get_hr() → use phs_metrics(data, metrics = "HR") get_or() → use phs_metrics(data, metrics = "", or_age = ...) get_cindex() → use phs_metrics(data, metrics = "C_index") get_hrsd() → use phs_metrics(data, metrics = "HR_SD") km_curve() → use phs_km()

title: hazrd 0.2.0
text: create_lookup_table() — superseded upcoming phs_abs_risk(). cum_inc() — scope package API. get_phs_age() — internal helper; re-exposed via phs_abs_risk().

title: hazrd 0.2.0
text: phs_metrics() metrics = "": or_age values outside observed KM time range now return NA informative warning instead propagating silent NA infinite odds (#-edge-case). phs_metrics() metrics = "HR_SD" bootstrap = TRUE: HR_SD now correctly included bootstrap statistic vector conf_low, conf_high, se populated.

title: hazrd 0.1.0
text: Initial release.
